input_summary.complaint: Several aircraft events per hour create low-frequency rumble and loud peaks that wake the family.
input_summary.hardware_assumption: future bedside quiet-zone hardware with local DSP

noise_profile.source_model: outdoor aircraft noise transmitted through building envelope with room reflections

near_field_anc.reason: Low-frequency event tails may be reduced near the pillow, but peaks are intermittent and require conservative handling.

passive_insulation.reason: Building envelope and window treatment are the primary controls for aircraft noise.

masking_sound.reason: Can reduce perceived contrast between quiet periods and aircraft events if kept below safe levels.

hearing_protection.reason: Optional sleep aid if the user can still detect safety alerts.

recommended_controls.target: aircraft noise entering through windows and vents
recommended_controls.reason: Passive controls reduce broad frequency content without relying on phase cancellation.
A limited quiet-zone strategy may help with predictable low-frequency portions of aircraft events.
Low-level masking can make intermittent events less startling when configured safely.

blocked_controls.reason: High-frequency and transient components are not reliable open-air ANC targets.

anc_policy.reason: Enable only for conservative low-frequency quiet-zone support, not for whole-room aircraft cancellation.
anc_policy.microphone_strategy: reference microphone near likely window path and error microphone near sleep position

rules: Do not promise full cancellation of aircraft events.

notes: Derived event features are sufficient for planning.

caveats: Aircraft noise can contain high-energy broadband peaks that active control may not handle.
Building envelope improvements may matter more than active control.
The plan requires calibrated hardware before any real acoustic output.
